title: map_put_error: 404 on write is mount-not-found, not NotFound — correct semantic split
description:
Why this choice is correct: Vault KV v2 write (POST /data/<path>) creates the secret at any path under a valid mount. A 404 on write means the mount doesn't exist, not the path. Returning NotFound (the SecretError variant for 'secret doesn't exist') would be a category error — callers use NotFound to decide whether to create a secret, and returning it on a bad mount would silently mislead them.

What a contributor might suggest: unify the two error mappers into one (DRY). This is wrong: the 404 semantics differ between read and write operations on KV v2. The duplication is load-bearing. The docstring on map_put_error explains why.
